c487ae.title: Link has non-empty accessible name
suggested_fixes: Provide a non-empty accessible name using visible text, an associated label, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`, depending on the control type.
Keep the programmatic name aligned with what users see on screen when the control has a visible label.

5c01ea.title: ARIA state or property is permitted
5c01ea.summary: ARIA state or property is permitted
suggested_fixes: Use only ARIA states and properties that are supported by the element's semantic role or are globally allowed.
If the required ARIA attribute is invalid for the current element, fix the role or remove the unsupported attribute rather than leaving conflicting semantics in place.

97a4e1.title: Button has non-empty accessible name
suggested_fixes: Give each button an accessible name from visible text, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
Prefer visible button text when possible so both visual and assistive-technology users get the same control name.
Provide a non-empty accessible name using visible text, an associated label, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`, depending on the control type.
Keep the programmatic name aligned with what users see on screen when the control has a visible label.

6cfa84.title: Element with aria-hidden has no content in sequential focus navigation
6cfa84.summary: Element with aria-hidden has no content in sequential focus navigation
suggested_fixes: Make sure users can reach the reported region or control using the keyboard without getting trapped or skipped.
Apply focusability only where it preserves a predictable tab sequence and exposes meaningful context.

e086e5.title: Form field has non-empty accessible name
e086e5.summary: Form field has non-empty accessible name
suggested_fixes: Associate each form control with a stable programmatic name using a `<label>`, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
Make sure the label describes the control's purpose, not only its current value or placeholder text.
Provide a non-empty accessible name using visible text, an associated label, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`, depending on the control type.
Keep the programmatic name aligned with what users see on screen when the control has a visible label.

cf77f2.title: Bypass Blocks of Repeated Content
suggested_fixes: Add a reliable skip mechanism or landmark path that lets keyboard users move past repeated content quickly.
Ensure the bypass target becomes focusable and visible when the skip mechanism is activated.
Add a working skip link or landmark path that lets keyboard users bypass repeated blocks of content.
Ensure the bypass target can receive focus and lands at the start of the main destination content.
Avoid failing patterns like: This HTML web page does not pass any of the input rules.
Use the ACT passing pattern as a reference: This HTML web page is passing rule Block of repeated content is collapsible.
implementation_notes: Passing ACT example: This HTML web page is passing rule Block of repeated content is collapsible.
Failing ACT example: This HTML web page does not pass any of the input rules.

09o5cg.title: Text has enhanced contrast
suggested_fixes: Adjust the text and background colors until the reported text meets the minimum contrast ratio in every visible state.
Re-test hover, focus, disabled, and placeholder states separately because color tokens often differ between states.
Adjust the foreground and background color combination until the reported content meets the required contrast ratio.
Check the failing text in every visible state, including hover, focus, disabled, and placeholder variants.
Avoid failing patterns like: This light gray text has a contrast ratio of 5.7:1 on the white background.
Use the ACT passing pattern as a reference: This dark gray text has a contrast ratio of 12.6:1 on the white background.
implementation_notes: Passing ACT example: This dark gray text has a contrast ratio of 12.6:1 on the white background.
Failing ACT example: This light gray text has a contrast ratio of 5.7:1 on the white background.

afw4f7.title: Text has minimum contrast
suggested_fixes: Increase contrast for all reported text, including smaller or lighter-weight variants that may fail before larger text does.
Avoid depending on font weight or size changes alone when the base color combination is still below the required ratio.
Adjust the foreground and background color combination until the reported content meets the required contrast ratio.
Check the failing text in every visible state, including hover, focus, disabled, and placeholder variants.
Avoid failing patterns like: This light gray text has a contrast ratio of 2.3:1 on the white background.
Use the ACT passing pattern as a reference: This dark gray text has a contrast ratio of 12.6:1 on the white background.
implementation_notes: Passing ACT example: This dark gray text has a contrast ratio of 12.6:1 on the white background.
Failing ACT example: This light gray text has a contrast ratio of 2.3:1 on the white background.

2ee8b8.title: Visible label is part of accessible name

suggested_fixes: Keep the accessible name aligned with the visible label so speech-input users can activate the control using the words they see on screen.
When extra context is needed in the accessible name, keep the full visible label intact at the start of that name.

bisz58.title: Meta element has no refresh delay (no exception)
bisz58.summary: Meta element has no refresh delay (no exception)
suggested_fixes: Avoid automatic redirects or refresh behavior that changes the page before users can read or interact with it.
If refresh behavior is required, provide user control and enough time to complete the current task.

bc659a.title: Meta element has no refresh delay
bc659a.summary: Meta element has no refresh delay
suggested_fixes: Remove viewport settings that prevent zoom, especially `user-scalable=no` and restrictive `maximum-scale` values.
Verify the page still works when users zoom to at least 200% on mobile browsers.
Avoid failing patterns like: This page refreshes after 30 seconds.
Use the ACT passing pattern as a reference: This page redirects to a new page immediately.
implementation_notes: Passing ACT example: This page redirects to a new page immediately.
Failing ACT example: This page refreshes after 30 seconds.

b4f0c3.title: Meta viewport allows for zoom
suggested_fixes: Remove restrictive viewport settings that block zoom or cap the usable scale too aggressively.
Confirm the page remains usable when zoomed without forcing two-dimensional scrolling for the core content.

307n5z.title: Element with presentational children has no focusable content
307n5z.summary: Element with presentational children has no focusable content
suggested_fixes: Make sure users can reach the reported region or control using the keyboard without getting trapped or skipped.
Apply focusability only where it preserves a predictable tab sequence and exposes meaningful context.

80f0bf.title: Audio or video element avoids automatically playing audio
80f0bf.summary: Audio or video element avoids automatically playing audio

8fc3b6.title: Object element rendering non-text content has non-empty accessible name
8fc3b6.summary: Object element rendering non-text content has non-empty accessible name
suggested_fixes: Provide a non-empty accessible name using visible text, an associated label, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`, depending on the control type.

46ca7f.title: Element marked as decorative is not exposed
46ca7f.summary: Element marked as decorative is not exposed
suggested_fixes: Hide only genuinely decorative content from assistive technology; otherwise provide a meaningful accessible name or text alternative.
Do not mark content as decorative when it conveys information, state, or a control purpose.

0ssw9k.title: Scrollable element is keyboard accessible
suggested_fixes: Make scrollable regions keyboard reachable so users can move focus into the region before attempting to scroll it.
Add focusability only where it preserves a predictable tab order and exposes meaningful context to assistive technology.
Make sure users can reach the reported region or control using the keyboard without getting trapped or skipped.
Apply focusability only where it preserves a predictable tab sequence and exposes meaningful context.
Avoid failing patterns like: This vertically scrollable section
implementation_notes: Failing ACT example: This vertically scrollable section

7d6734.title: SVG element with explicit role has non-empty accessible name
7d6734.summary: SVG element with explicit role has non-empty accessible name
suggested_fixes: Give SVG or graphics used as images an accessible name with visible text, `<title>`, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
If the graphic is purely decorative, remove it from the accessibility tree rather than leaving it unnamed.
Provide a non-empty accessible name using visible text, an associated label, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`, depending on the control type.
Keep the programmatic name aligned with what users see on screen when the control has a visible label.

a25f45.title: Headers attribute specified on a cell refers to cells in the same table element
a25f45.summary: Headers attribute specified on a cell refers to cells in the same table element
suggested_fixes: Use `headers` only when it reflects real data-to-header relationships in complex tables.
Prefer simpler semantic table structures with `th` and `scope` when they can express the same relationships clearly.
Use semantic table markup that accurately matches the relationship between headers and data cells.
Avoid adding header attributes unless the table structure truly requires them and the relationships are correct.

d0f69e.title: Table header cell has assigned cells
d0f69e.summary: Table header cell has assigned cells
suggested_fixes: Use header cells only when the table actually contains related data cells that depend on those headers.
Restructure layout tables into semantic content or a proper data table before adding more header markup.
Use semantic table markup that accurately matches the relationship between headers and data cells.
Avoid adding header attributes unless the table structure truly requires them and the relationships are correct.

de46e4.title: Element with lang attribute has valid language tag
de46e4.summary: Element with lang attribute has valid language tag
suggested_fixes: Set a valid language code that matches the actual language of the page or element content.
If both `lang` and `xml:lang` are present, keep them synchronized to avoid conflicting language metadata.
Set a valid language code that matches the actual language of the content being announced.
Keep page-level and element-level language metadata consistent when both are present.

eac66b.title: Video element auditory content has accessible alternative
eac66b.summary: Video element auditory content has accessible alternative
suggested_fixes: Avoid failing patterns like: A video element without any form of captions.
Use the ACT passing pattern as a reference: A video element with an associated track element that contains captions for all the audio.
implementation_notes: Passing ACT example: A video element with an associated track element that contains captions for all the audio.
Failing ACT example: A video element without any form of captions.
